edges.description: Impaired von Willebrand factor-dependent primary hemostasis produces mucosal bleeding including epistaxis.
edges.source: Von Willebrand Factor Deficiency or Dysfunction

edges.description: Defective primary hemostasis increases bruising susceptibility.
edges.source: Von Willebrand Factor Deficiency or Dysfunction

edges.description: Mucocutaneous bleeding tendency can manifest as heavy menstrual bleeding.
edges.source: Von Willebrand Factor Deficiency or Dysfunction

evidence.snippet: Characteristic bleeding symptoms include epistaxis, easy bruising, oral cavity bleeding, menorrhagia, bleeding after dental extraction, surgery, and/or childbirth, and in severe cases, bleeding into joints and soft tissues.

Characteristic bleeding symptoms include epistaxis, easy bruising, oral cavity bleeding, menorrhagia, bleeding after dental extraction, surgery, and/or childbirth, and in severe cases, bleeding into joints and soft tissues.

Characteristic bleeding symptoms include epistaxis, easy bruising, oral cavity bleeding, menorrhagia, bleeding after dental extraction, surgery, and/or childbirth, and in severe cases, bleeding into joints and soft tissues.

nodes.description: Deficiency or qualitative defects of von Willebrand factor impair primary hemostasis and predispose to mucocutaneous bleeding.
nodes.id: Von Willebrand Factor Deficiency or Dysfunction

evidence.snippet: von Willebrand disease is a common inherited bleeding disorder characterized by excessive mucocutaneous bleeding.
